title.emphasis: The Operational Layer: The MSA and SOW Framework

section.para: For engaging independent artists, a two-part structure consisting of a Master Service Agreement (MSA) and project-specific Statements of Work (SOWs) provides maximum efficiency and legal protection.1

listitem.para: Master Service Agreement (MSA): The Relationship Contract. The MSA is a foundational document that governs the overall legal relationship with an artist.1 It is signed once at the beginning of the engagement and sets the standing rules for all future work, covering crucial terms like confidentiality, liability, dispute resolution, and the default handling of intellectual property rights.1

Statement of Work (SOW): The Project Contract. The SOW is a shorter, project-specific document that operates under the umbrella of the MSA.1 Each new commission gets its own SOW, which details the specific scope, deliverables, timelines, and payment schedule for that particular project.1 This structure allows the organization to launch new projects quickly without renegotiating core legal terms each time.1
